label: The Witcher
blurb: The Continent, monsters and all. Quotes from the games, lore from the saga.

quotes.hint: The weather report, delivered a thousand times on the Path
Said to roughly half the bestiary

quotes.q: Evil is evil. Lesser, greater, middling, makes no difference.
quotes.hint: His whole moral philosophy in nine words

quotes.q: No witcher's ever died in his own bed.
quotes.hint: Cold comfort for Ciri after the siege of Kaer Morhen

quotes.q: There's nothing behind me. I'm a witcher, I'd have heard it.
quotes.hint: To a bandit trying the oldest trick there is

quotes.q: You were always an unruly child. I adored that about you. Now fly!
quotes.hint: An old master’s final words during the siege

quotes.q: Kings die, realms fall, but magic endures.
quotes.hint: The Lodge of Sorceresses’ worldview in seven words

quotes.q: If he thinks he can buy me, he is sorely mistaken.
quotes.hint: Storming out of a meeting with a certain Emperor
